## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1983
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded.
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data.
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)

wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1973
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded.
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data.
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event

event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data.
magnitude: FLOAT | "Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches"
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
magnitude_type: TEXT | "Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail"
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
damage_crops: NUMBER | "The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event"
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded.
cz_type: TEXT | "Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine"
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
damage_property: NUMBER | "The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event"
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
source: TEXT | "Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories"
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
tor_f_scale: TEXT | "Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)"
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event
cz_name: TEXT | "(County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones        "
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
cz_fips_code: TEXT | "Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties "
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
state_fips_code: TEXT | "Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states "
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1963
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded.
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches

tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood

event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event

reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined

The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data.
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.

event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state. State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size. Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded.
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured. - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.TORNADO_PATHS
magnitude: TEXT | EF-Scale rating for the tornado. Possible values are 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 , 5 and unknown
storm_date: DATE | Date of the tornado
time_zone_offset: TEXT | Timezone offset of where the the tornado occurred. Possible values are -06:00 (CST), -00:00 (GMT/UTC), and unknown
end_lat: FLOAT | Tornado's end latitude in decimal degrees
injured_count: NUMBER | Number of people injured as a result of the storm
yearly_tornado_count: TEXT | Count of tornadoes that year after the given tornado is reported to the NWS database. This does not necessarily indicate sequential order because of reporting potential delays.
end_long: FLOAT | Tornado's end longitude in decimal degrees
storm_time: TIME | Time of the tornado
length: FLOAT | Length of the tornado, in miles
crop_loss: TEXT | Estimated crop value loss in millions of USD. Note that zero does not indicate $0. This data was is available starting in 2007
width: FLOAT | Width of the tornado in feet
start_lon: FLOAT | Tornado's starting longitude in decimal degrees
tornado_path_geom: BINARY | Geographic representation of the storm's path
state_fips_code: TEXT | 2-digit state FIPS code. This maps to all state_fips_code columns in the BigQuery Public Datasets Program
state_abbreviation: TEXT | 2 letter postal abbreviation of the state's name
fatality_count: NUMBER | Number of fatalities that resulted from the storm
state_name: TEXT | Full text name of the state in which the tornado occurred
start_lat: FLOAT | Tornado's starting latitude in decimal degrees
property_loss: TEXT | Estimated property loss in USD. Note that zero does not indicate $0. Possible values are: 0 - unknown or not provided; 1 - <50; 2 - 50 - <500; 3 - 500 - <5,000; 4 - 5,000 - <50,000; 5 - 50,000 - <500,000; 6 - 500,000 - <5,000,000; 7 - 5,000,000 - <50,000,000; 8 - 50,000,000 - <500,000,000; 9 - 500,000,000 or greater.

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1962
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data.
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded.
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file

## NOAA_ICOADS.ICOADS_CORE_2016
wave_height: FLOAT | Height of wind waves, in metres.
dpt_indicator: NUMBER | WBTI and DPTI indicate which of WBT or DPT was measured or computed, and ice bulb conditions: 0 – measured 1 – computed 2 – iced measured 3 – iced computed
air_temperature: FLOAT | Air temperature, Celsius
wave_direction: NUMBER | Starting in 1968, WD was no longer reported in the SHIP code. Codes 00 to 36 (note: leading zero is omitted in IMMA) show the direction (if any) from which (wind) waves come, in tens of degrees (following WMO Code 0877; ref. Code and Range columns in Table D2). Codes 37 and 38 show: 37 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (WH ≤ 4.75 m) 38 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (WH > 4.75 m; or irrespective of wave height, corresponding to 99 in WMO Code 0877)
middle_cloud_type: TEXT | Codes 0 to 10 [A in base36 encoding] show characteristics observed of clouds of the genera Altocumulus, Altostratus, and Nimbostratus (WMO Code 0515).
year: NUMBER | Year of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. It is 4-digit
dewpoint_temperature: FLOAT | Dew-point temperature, Celsius
ship_speed: NUMBER | Ship’s average speed made good during the three hours preceding the time of observation (beginning 1 January 1968): 0 – 0 knots  1 – 1-5 knots 2 – 6-10 knots 3 – 11-15 knots 4 – 16-20 knots  5 – 21-25 knots 6 – 26-30 knots 7 – 31-35 knots 8 – 36-40 knots 9 – over 40 knots.    Prior to 1 January 1968 a different code for VS, also with range 0-9, applied (Met Office 1948): 0 – 0 knots 1 – 1-3 knots 2 – 4-6 knots 3 – 7-9 knots 4 – 10-12 knots 5 – 13-15 knots  6 – 16-18 knots 7 – 19-21 knots 8 – 22-24 knots 9 – over 24 knots
nightday_flag: NUMBER | The night/day report flag was set to indicate whether the report fell in local nighttime or daytime, as determined according to Slutz et al. 1 = report time is local nighttime 2 = report time is local daytime
release_status_indicator: NUMBER | An indicator that specifies whether the record is: 0 – Preliminary (Not yet included in an official ICOADS Release) 1 – Auxiliary (Records provided in separate data files in addition to ICOADS official Releases and Preliminary data. This also includes new data sources received, but awaiting blending into an official ICOADS Release) 2 – Full (A record included in an official ICOADS Release)
day: NUMBER | Day of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. Days 1-31
swell_height: FLOAT | Height of wind waves, in metres.
swell_period: NUMBER | Period of wind waves, in seconds. Starting in 1968, SP was reported in seconds; prior to 1968 the period was reported as a code, which was converted into whole seconds following Table D5a, with WX (C1, field 13) set accordingly.
wave_period_indicator: NUMBER | Indicates that the wave and swell periods were converted from code into whole seconds: 1 – period converted from code into whole seconds
intermediate_reject_flag: NUMBER | 0=Retain in Intermediate, Reject from Final dataset;  1=Retain in both Intermediate and Final datasets;  2=Reject from both Intermediate and Final datasets
sea_level_pressure: FLOAT | Amount of pressure tendency at station level during the three hours preceding the time of observation in tenths of hPa (i.e., millibars)
visibility_indicator: NUMBER | shows whether visibility was: 0 – estimated (or unknown method of observation) 1 – measured 2 – fog present (obsolete)
imma_version: NUMBER | IMMA Version: 0=2010, 1=2016
swell_period_indicator: NUMBER | Indicates that the wave and swell periods were converted from code into whole seconds: 1 – period converted from code into whole seconds
country_code: TEXT | The country that recruited a ship, which may differ from the country of immediate receipt (C2, field 15) and may also differ from the ship’s registry
timestamp: NUMBER | Converted UTC timestamp for the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read
present_weather: NUMBER | WMO Codes 4677 (Table D3) for WW, and 4561 for W1: 0 – Cloud covering 1/2 or less of the sky throughout the appropriate period 1 – Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky during part of the appropriate period and covering 1/2 or less during part of the period 2 – Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky throughout the appropriate period 54 3 – Sandstorm, duststorm or blowing snow 4 – Fog or ice fog or thick haze 5 – Drizzle 6 – Rain 7 – Snow, or rain and snow mixed 8 – Shower(s) 9 – Thunderstorm(s) with or without precipitation
dup_check: NUMBER | The presence of a duplicate match between a Global Telecommunication System (GTS) and logbook (or other delayed-mode) report may provide some location verification, with greater credibility if SLP and SST match under “allowances.” DUPC indicates whether such matches were detected during duplicate elimination processing (either the GTS or delayed-mode report is retained in the output data mixture), in case users might wish to make use of this information for independent quality control purposes: 0 – GTS and logbook match with SLP and SST match 1 – GTS and logbook match without SLP and SST match 2 – no GTS and logbook match was encountered
release_no_secondary: NUMBER | Second of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
sst_measurement_method: NUMBER | Shows the method by which SST was taken: 0 – bucket 1 – condenser inlet (intake) 2 – trailing thermistor 3 – hull contact sensor 4 – through hull sensor 5 – radiation thermometer 6 – bait tanks thermometer 7 – others 9 – unknown or non-bucket 10 – “implied” bucket [note: applicable to early ICOADS data] 11 – reversing thermometer or mechanical sensor 12 – electronic sensor
wind_direction_true: NUMBER | The direction (true) from which wind is blowing (or will blow), stored in whole degrees (i.e., 360-point compass; range: 1-360°), or special codes: 361 – calm 362 – variable, or all directions
sea_surface_temp: FLOAT | Temperatures are stored in tenths of a degree Celsius.
ncdc_qc_flags: TEXT | Possible NCDC-QC flag values, where “–” indicates an undefined flag value, or letters included in the table indicate a defined flag value. For the letters, lower-case “x” indicates a flag value not utilized, whereas upper-case flag values were utilized, in preparing enhanced or standard trimmed data
high_cloud_type: TEXT | Codes 0 to 10 [A in base36 encoding] show characteristics observed of clouds of the genera Cirrus, Cirrocumulus and Cirrostratus (WMO Code 0509).
id_indicator: NUMBER | ID Indicator indicates whether a callsign or some other sort of identification is contained in the ID field (and in R3.0 data, II should always be extant when ID information exists; whereas II should always be missing if ID is missing): 0 – ID present, but unknown type 1 – ship, Ocean Station Vessel (OSV), or ice station callsign 2 – generic ID (e.g., SHIP, BUOY, RIGG, PLAT) 3 – WMO 5-digit buoy number 4 – other buoy number (e.g., Argos or national buoy number) 5 – Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) ID (assigned by US NDBC or other organizations) 6 – station name or number 7 – oceanographic platform/cruise number 8 – fishing vessel psuedo-ID 9 – national ship number 10 – composite information from early ship data 11 – 7-digit buoy ID (proposed)
wetbulb_temperature: FLOAT | Wet-bulb temperature, Celsius
pressure_bias: NUMBER | Indicates questionable sea level pressure data: 0 – questionable SLP: level 0: individual platform (unused) 1 – questionable SLP: level 1: deck 2 – questionable SLP: level 2: deck
box_system_indicator: TEXT | The box system indicator is currently unused.
past_weather: NUMBER | WMO Codes 4677 (Table D3) for WW, and 4561 for W1: 0 – Cloud covering 1/2 or less of the sky throughout the appropriate period 1 – Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky during part of the appropriate period and covering 1/2 or less during part of the period 2 – Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky throughout the appropriate period 54 3 – Sandstorm, duststorm or blowing snow 4 – Fog or ice fog or thick haze 5 – Drizzle 6 – Rain 7 – Snow, or rain and snow mixed 8 – Shower(s) 9 – Thunderstorm(s) with or without precipitation
month: NUMBER | Month of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. (1=January, 2=February, …, 12=December)
indicator_for_temp: NUMBER | Indicator provides information about the precision and/or units that the Core temperature elements were translated from: 0 – tenths °C 1 – half °C 2 – whole °C 3 – whole or tenths °C (mixed precision among temperature fields) 4 – tenths °F 5 – half °F 6 – whole °F 7 – whole or tenths °F (mixed precision among temperature fields) 8 – high resolution data (e.g., hundredths °C) 9 – other
wind_speed: FLOAT | Wind speed which is stored in tenths of a meter per second (to retain adequate precision for winds converted from knots, or high-resolution data)
one_degree_box_number: NUMBER | 1° box numbers (see Release 1, supp. G; http://icoads.noaa.gov/Release_1/suppG.html) are available e.g., for use in sorting operations
source_exclusion_flags: NUMBER | The single extant value of the source exclusion flags is defined as 1 = data automatically disqualified from statistics They range from 0-31
dup_status: NUMBER | Indicates duplicate status. 0 unique 1 best duplicate 2 best duplicate with substitution
amt_pressure_tend: FLOAT | Amount of pressure tendency at station level during the three hours preceding the time of observation in tenths of hPa (i.e., millibars)
cloud_height_indicator: NUMBER | Shows if cloud height H was: 0 – estimated 1 – measured
longitude: FLOAT | Position to hundredths of a degree +N or –S (measured north or south of the equator) and +E or −W (measured east or west of the Greenwich Meridian).
landlocked_flag: NUMBER | The single extant value of the landlocked flag (LZ) is defined as 1 = report over land If LZ is missing, this indicates that the report falls over an ocean or coastal region as defined by a “landlocked” file at 2°×2° resolution
release_no_tertiary: NUMBER | Third of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
national_source_indicator: NUMBER | A field available for national use in identifying data subsets.
lower_cloud_amount: NUMBER | 0 – clear 1 – 1 okta or less, but not zero 2-6 – 2-6 oktas 7 – 7 oktas or more, but not 8 oktas 8 – 8 oktas 9 – sky obscured by fog and/or other meteorological phenomena
visibility: NUMBER | Visibility (horizontal visibility at the surface in kilometers) according to WMO Code 4377 from which, in reporting visibility at sea, WMO (2009a; Reg. 12.2.1.3.2) states that the decile 90-99 shall be used (moreover Reg. 12.2.1.3.1: when the horizontal visibility is not the same in different directions, the shortest distance shall be given for VV): 90 – less than 0.05 kilometer 91 – 0.05 92 – 0.2 93 – 0.5 94 – 1 95 – 2 96 – 4 97 – 10 98 – 20 99 – 50 or more
low_cloud_type: TEXT | Codes 0 to 10 [A in base36 encoding] show characteristics observed of clouds of the genera Stratocumulus, Stratus, Cumulus, and Cumulonimbus (WMO Code 0513
wind_speed_indicator: NUMBER | Wind speed is stored in tenths of a meter per second (to retain adequate precision for winds converted from knots, or high-resolution data). WI shows the units in which and/or the method by which W was originally recorded (0, 1, 3, 4 follow WMO Code 1855): 0 – meter per second, estimated 53 1 – meter per second, obtained from anemometer (measured) 2 – estimated (original units unknown) 3 – knot, estimated 4 – knot, obtained from anemometer (measured) 5 – Beaufort force (based on documentation) 6 – estimated (original units unknown)/unknown method 7 – measured (original units unknown) 8 – high-resolution measurement (e.g., hundredths of a meter per second)
cloud_height: TEXT | Codes 0 to 9 and “A” (following WMO Code 1600) show the height above surface of the base of the lowest cloud seen (such that a height exactly equal to one of the values at  60 the ends of the ranges shall be coded in the higher range, e.g., a height of 600 m shall be reported by code 5): 0 – 0 to 50 m 1 – 50 to 100 m 2 – 100 to 200 m 3 – 200 to 300 m 4 – 300 to 600 m 5 – 600 to 1000 m 6 – 1000 to 1500 m 7 – 1500 to 2000 m 8 – 2000 to 2500 m 9 – 2500 m or more, or no clouds 10 [A in base36 encoding] – height of base of cloud not known or base of clouds at a level lower and tops at a level higher than that of the station
track_check: NUMBER | Indicates if a report was: 0 – not track checked 1 – track checked
swell_direction: NUMBER | Starting in 1968, SD was no longer reported in the SHIP code. Codes 00 to 36 (note: leading zero is omitted in IMMA) show the direction (if any) from which (wind) waves come, in tens of degrees (following WMO Code 0877; ref. Code and Range columns in Table D2). Codes 37 and 38 show: 37 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (SH ≤ 4.75 m) 38 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (SH > 4.75 m; or irrespective of wave height, corresponding to 99 in WMO Code 0877)
deck: NUMBER | Number of the deck from which the report came (Table D6a), with Tables D6b and D6c providing additional information about selected DCK ranges. “Deck” originally referred to a punched card deck, but is now used as the primary field to track ICOADS data collections.
unique_report_id: TEXT | A unique ID for each record in ICOADS represented as a base36 number of length 6.
latlong_indicator: NUMBER | Latlong indicator preserves the precision at which LAT and LON were recorded or translated from, or if they were derived later by interpolation between known positions: 0 – degrees and tenths 1 – whole degrees 2 – mixed precision 3 – interpolated 4 – degrees and minutes 5 – high resolution data (e.g., degrees to seconds) 6 – other
ten_degree_box_number: NUMBER | 10° box numbers (see Release 1, supp. G; http://icoads.noaa.gov/Release_1/suppG.html) are available e.g., for use in sorting operations
source_id: NUMBER | Number of the source ID from which the report came (Table D7). Each SID may contain a single deck or a mixture of decks, but each SID is generally constrained to a single input format.
characteristic_of_ppp: NUMBER | WMO Code 0200 for characteristic of pressure tendency during the three hours preceding the time of observation (A) (after WMO 2015).
wbt_indicator: NUMBER | WBTI and DPTI indicate which of WBT or DPT was measured or computed, and ice bulb conditions: 0 – measured 1 – computed 2 – iced measured 3 – iced computed
ship_course: NUMBER | True direction of resultant displacement of the ship during the three hours preceding the time of observation (i.e., ship’s course (true) made good): 0 – stationary (ship hove to) 5 – SW 1 – NE 6 – W 2 – E 7 – NW 3 – SE 8 – N 4 – S 9 – unknown
external: NUMBER | The single extant value of the external flags is defined as 1 = erroneous (based on external OSD buoy quality control) They range from 0-63
release_no_primary: NUMBER | First of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
trimming_flags: TEXT | These flags indicate the relationship of a given observational data value to the legacy trimming limits, or to indicate if those limits, which were calculated separately for three historical periods are unavailable, or other conditions.
wave_period: NUMBER | Period of wind waves, in seconds. Starting in 1968, WP was reported in seconds; prior to 1968 the period was reported as a code, which was converted into whole seconds following Table D5a, with WX (C1, field 13) set accordingly.
total_cloud_amount: NUMBER | Codes 0 to 9 (WMO Code 2700) show the total fraction of the celestial dome covered by clouds (irrespective of their genus). 0 – clear 1 – 1 okta or less, but not zero 2-6 – 2-6 oktas 7 – 7 oktas or more, but not 8 oktas 8 – 8 oktas 9 – sky obscured by fog and/or other meteorological phenomena
wind_direction_indicator: NUMBER | Gives the compass (and approximate precision) used for reporting the wind direction: 0 – 36-point compass 1 – 32-point compass 2 – 16 of 36-point compass 3 – 16 of 32-point compass 4 – 8-point compass 5 – 360-point compass 6 – high resolution data (e.g., tenths of degrees)
callsign: TEXT | Identification/Callsign
adaptive_qc_flags: TEXT | SQZ indicates the relationship of SST to “adaptive” QC limits in 0.5σ (standard deviation) increments, and SQZ provides a measure of the reliability of the QC
second_country_code: NUMBER | The country of immediate receipt (C2), which may differ from the recruiting country (C1) and may also differ from the ship’s registry.
latitude: FLOAT | Position to hundredths of a degree +N or –S (measured north or south of the equator) and +E or −W (measured east or west of the Greenwich Meridian).
attm_count: NUMBER | The attm count: 0 – abbreviated record (no attm) 1 – one attm 2 – two attms etc.
hour: FLOAT | Hour of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. Hours 0.00 to 23.99
platform_type: NUMBER | The type of observing platform: 0 – US Navy or “deck” log, or unknown 1 – merchant ship or foreign military 2 – ocean station vessel—off station or station proximity unknown 3 – ocean station vessel—on station 4 – lightship 5 – ship 6 – moored buoy 7 – drifting buoy 8 – ice buoy [note: currently unused] 9 – ice station (manned, including ships overwintering in ice) 10 – oceanographic station data (bottle and low-resolution CTD/XCTD data) 11 – mechanical/digital/micro bathythermograph (MBT) 12 – expendable bathythermograph (XBT) 13 – Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) (NDBC operated) 14 – other coastal/island station 15 – fixed (or mobile) ocean platform (plat, rig) 16 – tide gauge 17 – high-resolution Conductivity-Temp.-Depth (CTD)/Expendable CTD (XCTD) 74 18 – profiling float 19 – undulating oceanographic recorder 20 – autonomous pinneped bathythermograph 21 – glider
time_indicator: NUMBER | Time indicator preserves the incoming precision of time fields: 0 – nearest whole hour 1 – hour to tenths 2 – hour plus minutes 3 – high resolution (e.g., hour to hundredths)

external: NUMBER | The single extant value of the external flags is defined as 1 = erroneous (based on external OSD buoy quality control) They range from 0-63
middle_cloud_type: TEXT | Codes 0 to 10 [A in base36 encoding] show characteristics observed of clouds of the genera Altocumulus, Altostratus, and Nimbostratus (WMO Code 0515).
swell_direction: NUMBER | Starting in 1968, SD was no longer reported in the SHIP code. Codes 00 to 36 (note: leading zero is omitted in IMMA) show the direction (if any) from which (wind) waves come, in tens of degrees (following WMO Code 0877; ref. Code and Range columns in Table D2). Codes 37 and 38 show: 37 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (SH ≤ 4.75 m) 38 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (SH > 4.75 m; or irrespective of wave height, corresponding to 99 in WMO Code 0877)
dewpoint_temperature: FLOAT | Dew-point temperature, Celsius
attm_count: NUMBER | The attm count: 0 – abbreviated record (no attm) 1 – one attm 2 – two attms etc.
source_id: NUMBER | Number of the source ID from which the report came (Table D7). Each SID may contain a single deck or a mixture of decks, but each SID is generally constrained to a single input format.
deck: NUMBER | Number of the deck from which the report came (Table D6a), with Tables D6b and D6c providing additional information about selected DCK ranges. “Deck” originally referred to a punched card deck, but is now used as the primary field to track ICOADS data collections.
sea_level_pressure: FLOAT | Amount of pressure tendency at station level during the three hours preceding the time of observation in tenths of hPa (i.e., millibars)
pressure_bias: NUMBER | Indicates questionable sea level pressure data: 0 – questionable SLP: level 0: individual platform (unused) 1 – questionable SLP: level 1: deck 2 – questionable SLP: level 2: deck
release_no_tertiary: NUMBER | Third of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
ship_speed: NUMBER | Ship’s average speed made good during the three hours preceding the time of observation (beginning 1 January 1968): 0 – 0 knots  1 – 1-5 knots 2 – 6-10 knots 3 – 11-15 knots 4 – 16-20 knots  5 – 21-25 knots 6 – 26-30 knots 7 – 31-35 knots 8 – 36-40 knots 9 – over 40 knots.    Prior to 1 January 1968 a different code for VS, also with range 0-9, applied (Met Office 1948): 0 – 0 knots 1 – 1-3 knots 2 – 4-6 knots 3 – 7-9 knots 4 – 10-12 knots 5 – 13-15 knots  6 – 16-18 knots 7 – 19-21 knots 8 – 22-24 knots 9 – over 24 knots
unique_report_id: TEXT | A unique ID for each record in ICOADS represented as a base36 number of length 6.
time_indicator: NUMBER | Time indicator preserves the incoming precision of time fields: 0 – nearest whole hour 1 – hour to tenths 2 – hour plus minutes 3 – high resolution (e.g., hour to hundredths)
track_check: NUMBER | Indicates if a report was: 0 – not track checked 1 – track checked
wave_period_indicator: NUMBER | Indicates that the wave and swell periods were converted from code into whole seconds: 1 – period converted from code into whole seconds
cloud_height: TEXT | Codes 0 to 9 and “A” (following WMO Code 1600) show the height above surface of the base of the lowest cloud seen (such that a height exactly equal to one of the values at  60 the ends of the ranges shall be coded in the higher range, e.g., a height of 600 m shall be reported by code 5): 0 – 0 to 50 m 1 – 50 to 100 m 2 – 100 to 200 m 3 – 200 to 300 m 4 – 300 to 600 m 5 – 600 to 1000 m 6 – 1000 to 1500 m 7 – 1500 to 2000 m 8 – 2000 to 2500 m 9 – 2500 m or more, or no clouds 10 [A in base36 encoding] – height of base of cloud not known or base of clouds at a level lower and tops at a level higher than that of the station
callsign: TEXT | Identification/Callsign
trimming_flags: TEXT | These flags indicate the relationship of a given observational data value to the legacy trimming limits, or to indicate if those limits, which were calculated separately for three historical periods are unavailable, or other conditions.
wetbulb_temperature: FLOAT | Wet-bulb temperature, Celsius
release_no_primary: NUMBER | First of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
national_source_indicator: NUMBER | A field available for national use in identifying data subsets.
day: NUMBER | Day of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. Days 1-31
latitude: FLOAT | Position to hundredths of a degree +N or –S (measured north or south of the equator) and +E or −W (measured east or west of the Greenwich Meridian).
country_code: TEXT | The country that recruited a ship, which may differ from the country of immediate receipt (C2, field 15) and may also differ from the ship’s registry
sst_measurement_method: NUMBER | Shows the method by which SST was taken: 0 – bucket 1 – condenser inlet (intake) 2 – trailing thermistor 3 – hull contact sensor 4 – through hull sensor 5 – radiation thermometer 6 – bait tanks thermometer 7 – others 9 – unknown or non-bucket 10 – “implied” bucket [note: applicable to early ICOADS data] 11 – reversing thermometer or mechanical sensor 12 – electronic sensor
swell_period_indicator: NUMBER | Indicates that the wave and swell periods were converted from code into whole seconds: 1 – period converted from code into whole seconds
latlong_indicator: NUMBER | Latlong indicator preserves the precision at which LAT and LON were recorded or translated from, or if they were derived later by interpolation between known positions: 0 – degrees and tenths 1 – whole degrees 2 – mixed precision 3 – interpolated 4 – degrees and minutes 5 – high resolution data (e.g., degrees to seconds) 6 – other
total_cloud_amount: NUMBER | Codes 0 to 9 (WMO Code 2700) show the total fraction of the celestial dome covered by clouds (irrespective of their genus). 0 – clear 1 – 1 okta or less, but not zero 2-6 – 2-6 oktas 7 – 7 oktas or more, but not 8 oktas 8 – 8 oktas 9 – sky obscured by fog and/or other meteorological phenomena

## NOAA_ICOADS.ICOADS_CORE_2012
characteristic_of_ppp: NUMBER | WMO Code 0200 for characteristic of pressure tendency during the three hours preceding the time of observation (A) (after WMO 2015).

ncdc_qc_flags: TEXT | Possible NCDC-QC flag values, where “–” indicates an undefined flag value, or letters included in the table indicate a defined flag value. For the letters, lower-case “x” indicates a flag value not utilized, whereas upper-case flag values were utilized, in preparing enhanced or standard trimmed data

visibility: NUMBER | Visibility (horizontal visibility at the surface in kilometers) according to WMO Code 4377 from which, in reporting visibility at sea, WMO (2009a; Reg. 12.2.1.3.2) states that the decile 90-99 shall be used (moreover Reg. 12.2.1.3.1: when the horizontal visibility is not the same in different directions, the shortest distance shall be given for VV): 90 – less than 0.05 kilometer 91 – 0.05 92 – 0.2 93 – 0.5 94 – 1 95 – 2 96 – 4 97 – 10 98 – 20 99 – 50 or more

WI shows the units in which and/or the method by which W was originally recorded (0, 1, 3, 4 follow WMO Code 1855): 0 – meter per second, estimated 53 1 – meter per second, obtained from anemometer (measured) 2 – estimated (original units unknown) 3 – knot, estimated 4 – knot, obtained from anemometer (measured) 5 – Beaufort force (based on documentation) 6 – estimated (original units unknown)/unknown method 7 – measured (original units unknown) 8 – high-resolution measurement (e.g., hundredths of a meter per second)

middle_cloud_type: TEXT | Codes 0 to 10 [A in base36 encoding] show characteristics observed of clouds of the genera Altocumulus, Altostratus, and Nimbostratus (WMO Code 0515).
sst_measurement_method: NUMBER | Shows the method by which SST was taken: 0 – bucket 1 – condenser inlet (intake) 2 – trailing thermistor 3 – hull contact sensor 4 – through hull sensor 5 – radiation thermometer 6 – bait tanks thermometer 7 – others 9 – unknown or non-bucket 10 – “implied” bucket [note: applicable to early ICOADS data] 11 – reversing thermometer or mechanical sensor 12 – electronic sensor
latlong_indicator: NUMBER | Latlong indicator preserves the precision at which LAT and LON were recorded or translated from, or if they were derived later by interpolation between known positions: 0 – degrees and tenths 1 – whole degrees 2 – mixed precision 3 – interpolated 4 – degrees and minutes 5 – high resolution data (e.g., degrees to seconds) 6 – other
dup_check: NUMBER | The presence of a duplicate match between a Global Telecommunication System (GTS) and logbook (or other delayed-mode) report may provide some location verification, with greater credibility if SLP and SST match under “allowances.” DUPC indicates whether such matches were detected during duplicate elimination processing (either the GTS or delayed-mode report is retained in the output data mixture), in case users might wish to make use of this information for independent quality control purposes: 0 – GTS and logbook match with SLP and SST match 1 – GTS and logbook match without SLP and SST match 2 – no GTS and logbook match was encountered
source_id: NUMBER | Number of the source ID from which the report came (Table D7). Each SID may contain a single deck or a mixture of decks, but each SID is generally constrained to a single input format.
swell_height: FLOAT | Height of wind waves, in metres.
imma_version: NUMBER | IMMA Version: 0=2010, 1=2016
national_source_indicator: NUMBER | A field available for national use in identifying data subsets.
platform_type: NUMBER | The type of observing platform: 0 – US Navy or “deck” log, or unknown 1 – merchant ship or foreign military 2 – ocean station vessel—off station or station proximity unknown 3 – ocean station vessel—on station 4 – lightship 5 – ship 6 – moored buoy 7 – drifting buoy 8 – ice buoy [note: currently unused] 9 – ice station (manned, including ships overwintering in ice) 10 – oceanographic station data (bottle and low-resolution CTD/XCTD data) 11 – mechanical/digital/micro bathythermograph (MBT) 12 – expendable bathythermograph (XBT) 13 – Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) (NDBC operated) 14 – other coastal/island station 15 – fixed (or mobile) ocean platform (plat, rig) 16 – tide gauge 17 – high-resolution Conductivity-Temp.-Depth (CTD)/Expendable CTD (XCTD) 74 18 – profiling float 19 – undulating oceanographic recorder 20 – autonomous pinneped bathythermograph 21 – glider
id_indicator: NUMBER | ID Indicator indicates whether a callsign or some other sort of identification is contained in the ID field (and in R3.0 data, II should always be extant when ID information exists; whereas II should always be missing if ID is missing): 0 – ID present, but unknown type 1 – ship, Ocean Station Vessel (OSV), or ice station callsign 2 – generic ID (e.g., SHIP, BUOY, RIGG, PLAT) 3 – WMO 5-digit buoy number 4 – other buoy number (e.g., Argos or national buoy number) 5 – Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) ID (assigned by US NDBC or other organizations) 6 – station name or number 7 – oceanographic platform/cruise number 8 – fishing vessel psuedo-ID 9 – national ship number 10 – composite information from early ship data 11 – 7-digit buoy ID (proposed)
visibility_indicator: NUMBER | shows whether visibility was: 0 – estimated (or unknown method of observation) 1 – measured 2 – fog present (obsolete)
release_no_primary: NUMBER | First of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
swell_period_indicator: NUMBER | Indicates that the wave and swell periods were converted from code into whole seconds: 1 – period converted from code into whole seconds
country_code: TEXT | The country that recruited a ship, which may differ from the country of immediate receipt (C2, field 15) and may also differ from the ship’s registry
box_system_indicator: TEXT | The box system indicator is currently unused.
source_exclusion_flags: NUMBER | The single extant value of the source exclusion flags is defined as 1 = data automatically disqualified from statistics They range from 0-31
release_status_indicator: NUMBER | An indicator that specifies whether the record is: 0 – Preliminary (Not yet included in an official ICOADS Release) 1 – Auxiliary (Records provided in separate data files in addition to ICOADS official Releases and Preliminary data. This also includes new data sources received, but awaiting blending into an official ICOADS Release) 2 – Full (A record included in an official ICOADS Release)
wave_period: NUMBER | Period of wind waves, in seconds. Starting in 1968, WP was reported in seconds; prior to 1968 the period was reported as a code, which was converted into whole seconds following Table D5a, with WX (C1, field 13) set accordingly.
day: NUMBER | Day of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. Days 1-31
latitude: FLOAT | Position to hundredths of a degree +N or –S (measured north or south of the equator) and +E or −W (measured east or west of the Greenwich Meridian).
sea_surface_temp: FLOAT | Temperatures are stored in tenths of a degree Celsius.
wind_direction_true: NUMBER | The direction (true) from which wind is blowing (or will blow), stored in whole degrees (i.e., 360-point compass; range: 1-360°), or special codes: 361 – calm 362 – variable, or all directions
wind_direction_indicator: NUMBER | Gives the compass (and approximate precision) used for reporting the wind direction: 0 – 36-point compass 1 – 32-point compass 2 – 16 of 36-point compass 3 – 16 of 32-point compass 4 – 8-point compass 5 – 360-point compass 6 – high resolution data (e.g., tenths of degrees)
adaptive_qc_flags: TEXT | SQZ indicates the relationship of SST to “adaptive” QC limits in 0.5σ (standard deviation) increments, and SQZ provides a measure of the reliability of the QC
total_cloud_amount: NUMBER | Codes 0 to 9 (WMO Code 2700) show the total fraction of the celestial dome covered by clouds (irrespective of their genus). 0 – clear 1 – 1 okta or less, but not zero 2-6 – 2-6 oktas 7 – 7 oktas or more, but not 8 oktas 8 – 8 oktas 9 – sky obscured by fog and/or other meteorological phenomena
year: NUMBER | Year of the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read. It is 4-digit
timestamp: NUMBER | Converted UTC timestamp for the actual time of observation at which the barometer is read
release_no_tertiary: NUMBER | Third of the three elements that make up the full release number associated with the record. For example, Release 3.0.1 is represented with RN1=3, RN2=0, and RN3=1
amt_pressure_tend: FLOAT | Amount of pressure tendency at station level during the three hours preceding the time of observation in tenths of hPa (i.e., millibars)
wetbulb_temperature: FLOAT | Wet-bulb temperature, Celsius
callsign: TEXT | Identification/Callsign
wave_direction: NUMBER | Starting in 1968, WD was no longer reported in the SHIP code. Codes 00 to 36 (note: leading zero is omitted in IMMA) show the direction (if any) from which (wind) waves come, in tens of degrees (following WMO Code 0877; ref. Code and Range columns in Table D2). Codes 37 and 38 show: 37 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (WH ≤ 4.75 m) 38 – waves confused, direction indeterminate (WH > 4.75 m; or irrespective of wave height, corresponding to 99 in WMO Code 0877)
past_weather: NUMBER | WMO Codes 4677 (Table D3) for WW, and 4561 for W1: 0 – Cloud covering 1/2 or less of the sky throughout the appropriate period 1 – Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky during part of the appropriate period and covering 1/2 or less during part of the period 2 – Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky throughout the appropriate period 54 3 – Sandstorm, duststorm or blowing snow 4 – Fog or ice fog or thick haze 5 – Drizzle 6 – Rain 7 – Snow, or rain and snow mixed 8 – Shower(s) 9 – Thunderstorm(s) with or without precipitation
intermediate_reject_flag: NUMBER | 0=Retain in Intermediate, Reject from Final dataset;  1=Retain in both Intermediate and Final datasets;  2=Reject from both Intermediate and Final datasets
track_check: NUMBER | Indicates if a report was: 0 – not track checked 1 – track checked
indicator_for_temp: NUMBER | Indicator provides information about the precision and/or units that the Core temperature elements were translated from: 0 – tenths °C 1 – half °C 2 – whole °C 3 – whole or tenths °C (mixed precision among temperature fields) 4 – tenths °F 5 – half °F 6 – whole °F 7 – whole or tenths °F (mixed precision among temperature fields) 8 – high resolution data (e.g., hundredths °C) 9 – other
visibility: NUMBER | Visibility (horizontal visibility at the surface in kilometers) according to WMO Code 4377 from which, in reporting visibility at sea, WMO (2009a; Reg. 12.2.1.3.2) states that the decile 90-99 shall be used (moreover Reg. 12.2.1.3.1: when the horizontal visibility is not the same in different directions, the shortest distance shall be given for VV): 90 – less than 0.05 kilometer 91 – 0.05 92 – 0.2 93 – 0.5 94 – 1 95 – 2 96 – 4 97 – 10 98 – 20 99 – 50 or more
swell_period: NUMBER | Period of wind waves, in seconds. Starting in 1968, SP was reported in seconds; prior to 1968 the period was reported as a code, which was converted into whole seconds following Table D5a, with WX (C1, field 13) set accordingly.
wind_speed_indicator: NUMBER | Wind speed is stored in tenths of a meter per second (to retain adequate precision for winds converted from knots, or high-resolution data). WI shows the units in which and/or the method by which W was originally recorded (0, 1, 3, 4 follow WMO Code 1855): 0 – meter per second, estimated 53 1 – meter per second, obtained from anemometer (measured) 2 – estimated (original units unknown) 3 – knot, estimated 4 – knot, obtained from anemometer (measured) 5 – Beaufort force (based on documentation) 6 – estimated (original units unknown)/unknown method 7 – measured (original units unknown) 8 – high-resolution measurement (e.g., hundredths of a meter per second)
unique_report_id: TEXT | A unique ID for each record in ICOADS represented as a base36 number of length 6.
dpt_indicator: NUMBER | WBTI and DPTI indicate which of WBT or DPT was measured or computed, and ice bulb conditions: 0 – measured 1 – computed 2 – iced measured 3 – iced computed
dewpoint_temperature: FLOAT | Dew-point temperature, Celsius
trimming_flags: TEXT | These flags indicate the relationship of a given observational data value to the legacy trimming limits, or to indicate if those limits, which were calculated separately for three historical periods are unavailable, or other conditions.
one_degree_box_number: NUMBER | 1° box numbers (see Release 1, supp. G; http://icoads.noaa.gov/Release_1/suppG.html) are available e.g., for use in sorting operations
low_cloud_type: TEXT | Codes 0 to 10 [A in base36 encoding] show characteristics observed of clouds of the genera Stratocumulus, Stratus, Cumulus, and Cumulonimbus (WMO Code 0513
external: NUMBER | The single extant value of the external flags is defined as 1 = erroneous (based on external OSD buoy quality control) They range from 0-63
pressure_bias: NUMBER | Indicates questionable sea level pressure data: 0 – questionable SLP: level 0: individual platform (unused) 1 – questionable SLP: level 1: deck 2 – questionable SLP: level 2: deck
cloud_height: TEXT | Codes 0 to 9 and “A” (following WMO Code 1600) show the height above surface of the base of the lowest cloud seen (such that a height exactly equal to one of the values at  60 the ends of the ranges shall be coded in the higher range, e.g., a height of 600 m shall be reported by code 5): 0 – 0 to 50 m 1 – 50 to 100 m 2 – 100 to 200 m 3 – 200 to 300 m 4 – 300 to 600 m 5 – 600 to 1000 m 6 – 1000 to 1500 m 7 – 1500 to 2000 m 8 – 2000 to 2500 m 9 – 2500 m or more, or no clouds 10 [A in base36 encoding] – height of base of cloud not known or base of clouds at a level lower and tops at a level higher than that of the station
wbt_indicator: NUMBER | WBTI and DPTI indicate which of WBT or DPT was measured or computed, and ice bulb conditions: 0 – measured 1 – computed 2 – iced measured 3 – iced computed
ship_speed: NUMBER | Ship’s average speed made good during the three hours preceding the time of observation (beginning 1 January 1968): 0 – 0 knots  1 – 1-5 knots 2 – 6-10 knots 3 – 11-15 knots 4 – 16-20 knots  5 – 21-25 knots 6 – 26-30 knots 7 – 31-35 knots 8 – 36-40 knots 9 – over 40 knots.    Prior to 1 January 1968 a different code for VS, also with range 0-9, applied (Met Office 1948): 0 – 0 knots 1 – 1-3 knots 2 – 4-6 knots 3 – 7-9 knots 4 – 10-12 knots 5 – 13-15 knots  6 – 16-18 knots 7 – 19-21 knots 8 – 22-24 knots 9 – over 24 knots

## NOAA_ICOADS.ICOADS_CORE_2007
